questions.question: Question count: 20 Note: Best-effort structured extraction from a column-formatted source PDF. Where the source itself marked an answer as changed, that note is preserved.

Q1. (2024) Consider the following statements regarding World Toilet Organization: [2024] 1. It is one of the agencies of the United Nations. 2. World Toilet Summit, World Toilet Day and World Toilet College are the initiatives of this organization, to inspire action to tackle the global sanitation crisis. 3. The main focus of its function is to grant funds to the least developed countries and developing countries to achieve the end of open defecation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Source ref: intl_orgs / Q3
Q2. (2023) In the recent years Chad, Guinea, Mali and Sudan caught the international attention for which one of the following reasons common to all of them? [2023] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q13

options.A: Discovery of rich deposits of rare earth elements

questions.question: Q3. (2023) Consider the following statements: [2023] Statement-I: According to the United Nations" World Water Development Report, 2022', India extracts more than a quarter of the world's groundwater withdrawal each year. Statement-II: India needs to extract more than a quarter of the world's groundwater each year to satisfy the drinking water and sanitation needs of almost 18% of world's population living in its territory. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? Source ref: intl_orgs / Q8

options.A: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement- I
options.B: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.C: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.D: Statement-l is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

questions.question: Q4. (2022) Which one of the following best describes the term “greenwashing”? [2022] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q25

options.A: Conveying a false impression that a company’s products are eco-friendly and environmentally sound
options.B: Non-inclusion of ecological/environmental costs in the Annual Financial Statements of a country
options.C: Ignoring the disastrous ecological consequences while undertaking infrastructure development
options.D: Making mandatory provisions for environmental costs in a government project/programme

questions.question: Q5. (2021) Consider the following statements: [2021] Statement 1: The United Nations Capital Development Fund (UNCDF) and the Arbor Day Foundation have recently recognized Hyderabad as 2020 Tree City of the World. Statement 2: Hyderabad was selected for the recognition for a year following its commitment to grow and maintain the urban forests. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements? Source ref: intl_orgs / Q16

options.A: Both Statement1 and Statement 2 are correct and Statement 2 is the correct and Statement 2 is the correct explanation for Statement 1
options.B: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation for Statement 1
options.C: Statement 1 is correct but Statement 2 is not correct
options.D: Statement 1 is not correct but Statement 2 is correct

questions.question: Q6. (2016) What is/are the importance/importances of the 'United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification'? [2016] 1. It aims to promote effective action through innovative national programmes and supportive inter-national partnerships. 2. It has a special/particular focus on South Asia and North Africa regions, and its Secretariat facilitates the allocation of major portion of financial resources to these regions. 3. It is committed to bottom-up approach, encouraging the participation of local people in combating the desertification. Select the correct answer using the code given below. Source ref: intl_orgs / Q24

Q7. (2015) In the context of modern scientific research, consider the following statements about ‘IceCube’, a particle detector located at South Pole, which was recently in the news: [2015] 1. It is the world’s largest neutrino detector, encompassing a cubic kilometre of ice. 2. It is a powerful telescope to search for dark matter 3. It is buried deep in the ice. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q57

Q8. (2014) Consider the following international agreements: [2014] 1. The international Treaty on Plant Genetic Resources for Food and Agriculture. 2. The United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification. 3. The World Heritage Convention Which of the above has/have a bearing on the biodiversity? Source ref: intl_orgs / Q36

Q9. (2014) Consider the following statements regarding 'Earth Hour': [2014] 1. It is an initiative of UNEP and UNESCO 2. It is a movement in which the participants switch off the lights for one hour on a certain day every year. 3. It is a movement to raise the awareness about the climate change and the need to save the planet. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? Source ref: intl_orgs / Q37

Q10. (2010) Stiglitz Commission established by the President of the United Nations General Assembly was in the international news. The commission was supposed to deal with: [2010] Source ref: intl_orgs / Q49

options.A: The challenges posed by the impending global climate change and prepare a road m
options.B: The workings of the global financial systems and to explore ways and means to secure a more sustainable global order
options.C: Global terrorism and prepare a global action plan for the mitigation of terrorism
options.D: Expansion of the United Nations Security Council in the present global scenario

questions.question: Q11. (2010) In the context of the affairs of which of the following is the phrase “Special Safeguard Mechanisms” mentioned in the news frequently? [2010] Source ref: intl_orgs / Q51

Q12. (2009) In the middle of the year 2008 the Parliament of which one of the following countries became the first in the world to enact a Climate act by passing ''The Climate Change accountability Bill"? [2009] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q8
Q13. (2007) Where is Copacabana Beach located? [2007] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q88

Q14. (2007) With reference to the international meeting held in the year 2006, which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched? [2007] 1. NAM Summit: Havana 2. APEC Meeting: Bangkok 3. EU-India Summit: Helsinki 4. Un Climate Change Conference: Geneva Select the correct answer using the codes given below: Source ref: gk_part2 / Q99

Q15. (2005) Which pairs are correctly matched? [2005] 1. Champadevi Shukla: Goldman Environmental prize 2. Dr. P. Sri Ramachanrudu: Vachaspati Puraskar 3. Ela Ramesh Bhatt: Lal Bahadur Shastri National Award for Excellence in Public Administration, Academics and Management 4. Upamanyu Chatterjee: Lalit Kala Ratna Award Select the correct answer: Source ref: gk_part3 / Q55
Q16. (2004) The Ramon Magsaysay Award winner Shanta Sinha is known as: [2004] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q89

options.C: An organiser of rain-water harvesting schemes
options.D: An activist for the welfare of poor rural women

questions.question: Q17. (2004) Match List-I (Agency) with List-II (Headquarters) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the Lists: [2004] List-I (Agency) List-II (Headquarters ) A. United Nations Development Programme 1. Nairobi B. United Nations Environment Programme 2. Vienna C. United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) 3. Berne D. Universal Postal Union (UPU) 4. New York A B C D Source ref: intl_orgs / Q73
Q18. (1998) Indonesian forest fire in 1997 was caused by: [1998] Source ref: gk_part4 / Q21

Q19. (1998) Forest areas have been labelled as 1, 2, 3 and 4 in the rough outline map given: [1998] Among these, those which were threatened in 1997 by a serious epidemic include Source ref: gk_part4 / Q32

options.B: Oaks forests of 1 and sal forests of 2

questions.question: Q20. (1995) 'Monoculture of commercially viable trees is destroying the unique natural profile of ............Thoughtless exploitation of timber, deforesting vast tracts for palm cultivation, destruction of mangroves, illegal logging by tribals and poaching only compound the problem. Fresh water pockets are fast drying up due to deforestation and destruction of mangroves.' The place referred to in this quotation is: [1995] Source ref: gk_part4 / Q61
